The event is a 64-player single-elimination heads-up bracket, giving an equal-skill baseline of just 1.56%, and Feldman drew Sean Winter—a much stronger tournament specialist—in round one. Reporting before the cutoff also indicated leaked/insider-informed market odds had Sam Soverel above 90%, with only Brian Rast, Erik Seidel, and Sean Winter above 1%; Feldman was not among the plausible contenders. Taken together, the evidence suggests Feldman is very unlikely to be the eventual champion. I’m submitting the floor-compatible estimate of 5%.

As of the 2025-11-09 cutoff, DeepSeek does not lead Chatbot Arena: evidence indicates Gemini 2.5 Pro was #1 around early November at about 1451, while DeepSeek-v3.2-exp-thinking was around 1420, with Anthropic models also ahead. So DeepSeek likely needs a new late-2025 model release to win by Dec. 31. There were credible reports in September that DeepSeek was targeting an end-2025 next-generation/agentic model, but DeepSeek had already delayed R2-type plans multiple times and its September V3.2-Exp was described as an intermediate step rather than a clear frontier leap. Even if DeepSeek ships a new model before year-end, it must both enter Arena quickly and beat strong incumbents from Google/Anthropic/OpenAI. The alphabetical tiebreak helps DeepSeek versus Google/OpenAI/xAI, but not versus Anthropic, and only matters in exact ties. Net: meaningful but clearly sub-50 chance, best estimate about 18%.

OpenAI appears to have had the edge on Chatbot Arena in mid-2025: search evidence indicates GPT-5 topped the overall leaderboard in August, with OpenAI models also near the top in other categories. I found no clear pre-2025-11-09 evidence that Google, Anthropic, or xAI had decisively taken over by early November. That said, the frontier leaderboard in 2025 has been highly volatile, with major labs releasing updates every few weeks, and OpenAI only had a modest reported edge over Gemini/Claude rather than a runaway lead. The remaining horizon from Nov. 9 to Dec. 31 is short enough that incumbency matters, but long enough for one more strong Google or Anthropic release to overtake; ties also slightly hurt OpenAI because Google and Anthropic win alphabetically. Balancing likely current leadership against high year-end churn, I put OpenAI just under even odds.

By the 2025-11-09 cutoff, the evidence points strongly toward No. NASA materials are treating A11pl3Z / 3I/ATLAS as a natural interstellar comet, with Hubble/JWST observations showing a dust coma and CO2-rich outgassing, i.e. normal comet-like behavior rather than technology (search_1_summary). Reporting before the cutoff says NASA had already debunked claims that it was alien-made, and SETI-affiliated criticism emphasized the lack of non-gravitational acceleration as evidence against artificial propulsion (search_2_summary). Just as importantly, US agencies use a very high evidentiary bar for astrobiological claims, explicitly stressing that extraordinary evidence is required (search_1_summary). For this market to resolve Yes, a very senior US official or federal agency would need to definitively state that the object contains or is extraterrestrial life/technology by 2025-12-31. Given the existing official stance, the short remaining time window after Nov. 9, and the institutional caution around such claims, that looks extremely unlikely. I assign the floor-like 5% mainly to residual tail risk of a surprise late-2025 observation or an unexpected political/agency statement, not because the base evidence supports alien origin.

As of 2025-11-09, the Fed had made only two 25 bp-equivalent cuts in 2025: the first cut of the year in September and another 25 bp cut on October 29, leaving the target range at 3.75%-4.00% (search_0_summary; search_4_summary). With only the December 10 FOMC meeting remaining, this market can resolve YES only if the Fed delivers a 50 bp cut in December, because a 25 bp cut would bring the yearly total to 3 cuts and a hold would leave it at 2. Contemporary market pricing and reporting strongly argued against that outcome: Reuters and other summaries said December itself was not assured, CME/FedWatch-type pricing put the December 25 bp cut around 64%-68%, and separate reporting cited essentially 0% chance of a 50 bp move (search_1 result 0; search_4_summary). Given the hawkish tone, sticky inflation, and internal divisions, a 50 bp December cut looked like only a small tail risk. I therefore put the probability at the floor-compatible low end: 5%.

Portland looked like a clear pre-tournament long shot: most previews had them as a roughly .500-or-worse team (around a 35.5 win total), with Damian Lillard out for the season and a very weak offensive outlook, and West Group C was widely described as one of the toughest groups, featuring the Rockets, Nuggets, Warriors and Spurs. The main bullish update by Nov. 9 is that the Blazers already won their NBA Cup opener, beating Denver 109-107, so they are not drawing dead and their knockout-stage odds are meaningfully better than preseason. But they still likely need to survive a brutal group, probably via at least a 3-1 record and decent point differential, and then win three straight knockout games against stronger opponents. Net: they have a real path, but remain a major underdog.

By the Nov. 9, 2025 cutoff, no US ally appears close to meeting the resolution standard of independently possessing an operational nuclear weapon, and only ~7 weeks remain. Reporting through the cutoff shows policy debate and hedging in Japan, South Korea, Poland, and others, but no active weapon acquisition likely to finish by year-end; multiple experts explicitly argued the 2025 timeline does not work. The only somewhat live tail risk is Saudi Arabia after its September 2025 defense pact with Pakistan, but available Reuters/analysis describe this as a nuclear umbrella or extended deterrence arrangement, not transfer of full independent control, which the market explicitly excludes. Because covert transfer plus later credible confirmation is still not literally impossible, I assign a small residual YES chance, but it is very low.

As of 2025-11-09, NATO-Russia tensions had clearly risen: Poland/NATO had already shot down Russian drones in/near Polish airspace in September 2025, there were repeated Russian airspace incursions and many additional incidents across the Baltic/Nordic region, and NATO officials said Russia would likely keep testing boundaries. That raises the risk of a qualifying clash over the remaining ~4.5 months.

However, the resolution criteria are narrower than generic ‘escalation’: warning shots, collisions, cyberattacks, and interceptions of one-way attack drones/missiles aimed at a third party do NOT count. The most prominent kinetic incident before the cutoff appears to have involved Russian strike drones during attacks on Ukraine, which may well be excluded. Despite years of high tension since 2022, both sides had still shown strong incentives to avoid a direct exchange of fire between their regular forces.

So the event is materially possible—especially via a shootdown of a Russian non-munition UAV or a border/air/naval incident—but still remains more likely NO than YES by March 31, 2026.

As of 2025-11-09, Apple appears to have already released a new Vision Pro model. Official Apple sources identify an 'Apple Vision Pro (M5)' with 'Year introduced: 2025' and a distinct model number from the 2024 M2 version (Apple Support page, published Oct. 21, 2025). Multiple pre-cutoff credible reports say Apple announced the updated Vision Pro on Oct. 15, 2025 and made it available to customers/on sale on Oct. 22, 2025. That strongly satisfies the 'available for purchase by the general public' requirement. The main remaining uncertainty is interpretation: whether this M5 model is considered a true successor under the market’s wording, rather than just a spec bump. But the resolution criteria explicitly allow a successor released under a name other than 'Vision Pro 2,' and Apple’s own materials treat the M5 device as a new 2025 Vision Pro model. So YES is highly likely, though not absolutely certain because of that naming/qualification ambiguity.

As of 2025-11-09, the Eagles look like one of the NFL’s top contenders but not an overwhelming favorite. Evidence before the cutoff points to a 6-2 record, a strong lead in the NFC East, elite roster quality, and trade-deadline additions (notably defensive help) that improved their championship profile. Independent betting/odds coverage through Oct.-Nov. 2025 consistently had Philadelphia among the very shortest Super Bowl prices, roughly in the co-favorite/top-tier range. That said, a Super Bowl future still has to survive playoff variance, and there were several credible peers: Lions/Seahawks in the NFC and Bills/Ravens/Chiefs in the AFC. Injury risk around key contributors also slightly tempers the outlook. Net: clearly above the field average and one of the most likely single teams, but still only about a 1-in-7 chance overall.

By the Nov. 9, 2025 cutoff, there had been no divorce announcement from Donald Trump, Melania Trump, or their representatives, and multiple prior viral claims were fact-checked as false. The main pro-YES evidence is circumstantial: Melania’s very limited White House presence (NYT reported fewer than 14 days in the first 108 days), persistent public speculation, and Michael Wolff’s claim that they are effectively ‘separated.’ But that reporting was directly denied by the White House, there were still some joint public appearances, and there were no verified legal filings or direct statements indicating an imminent split. Most importantly, the forecast horizon remaining is short—only about seven weeks until Dec. 31—and this market requires an actual announcement, not just rumors of strain. Given a seemingly unconventional marriage but strong political/personal incentives not to publicly announce a divorce while Trump is president, I put the chance of a qualifying announcement before year-end at 7%.

By the Nov. 9, 2025 cutoff, Musk’s only clearly formal Trump-administration role had ended on May 30, before this market’s window. After that, reporting described him only as an informal/close adviser, which would not count under the resolution criteria. The strongest later evidence points away from a formal return: the sharp public Trump-Musk feud in early June, Trump saying he had no plans to speak to Musk and warning of consequences, and Musk later launching an “America Party.” I found no White House announcement or credible reporting before the cutoff showing any new nomination or appointment for Musk during June 4–Nov. 9. With no appointment yet evident and only ~7 weeks left in the year, a surprise formal reappointment is still possible but unlikely.

Stephen Chidwick was a confirmed entrant in the 64-player single-elimination $25K heads-up field, and he is one of the strongest pure poker players in the bracket. That pushes him well above the naive 1/64 baseline. However, even elite players have limited edge in short heads-up NLHE matches, and six straight match wins are needed. The available pre-2025-11-09 evidence I found confirms his participation and strong 2025 form, but does not directly identify him as the leaked/known winner. So I anchor mostly on a skill-based prior: roughly a several-times-average chance, but still in the single digits because of format variance. Net estimate: about 7%.

As of the Nov. 9, 2025 cutoff, this condition appears to have already been met. Multiple credible reports, including Reuters, describe Israeli air strikes beginning June 13, 2025 that hit targets on Iranian soil, including the Natanz nuclear site, air defense arrays in Tehran, and other military/energy targets (search_0_summary). Those are Israeli-initiated aerial strikes impacting Iranian ground territory, which matches the market’s YES criteria. I leave a small residual chance for resolution-source ambiguity or technical disputes about classification, but based on the available reporting, YES is overwhelmingly likely.

By the Nov. 9, 2025 cutoff, the weight of evidence points strongly to NO. Multiple independent sources available before cutoff—Copernicus, WMO, and Carbon Brief—said 2025 was virtually certain or very likely to finish only 2nd or 3rd warmest, behind 2024. The first half of 2025 was already about 0.08°C below the same period in 2024, and later months such as October ranked only around 3rd warmest rather than record warm. With only Nov-Dec left, NASA GISTEMP would need an unusually large late-year outperformance versus 2024 to overturn the deficit, and a weak La Niña backdrop makes that less likely. I leave a small residual chance for dataset-specific differences and an unexpectedly extreme Nov-Dec, but the probability that NASA’s 2025 annual anomaly ends up above 2024 is very low.

Highest 2 Lowest looks like a very weak candidate for Best Makeup and Hairstyling as of 2025-11-09. In the evidence I found, it is absent from category-specific prediction coverage, while films like Frankenstein and Wicked: For Good are explicitly discussed as major contenders. Reviews and awards-buzz coverage for Highest 2 Lowest focus on Denzel Washington, Spike Lee, and the film overall—not on prosthetics, creature work, or standout transformative makeup, which are often what drive this category. Since the market resolves No immediately if the film misses the nomination list, the key question is nomination viability, and that appears very low; even conditional on a nomination, it would still be an underdog versus stronger technical showcases. I would put the true chance below this if unconstrained, but under the required probability bounds I submit 5%.

By the Nov. 9, 2025 cutoff, gold was already trading around $4,000/oz and had recently broken above $4,100, leaving a roughly $800 buffer over the $3,200 threshold. For this market to resolve NO, gold would need a drop of more than 20% in under two months. That is possible in a volatile commodity, but it would be a very large reversal from then-current levels. The pre-cutoff evidence also points to continued support: Reuters reported persistent safe-haven demand, expectations of Fed easing, and strong analyst outlooks, including Goldman Sachs at $3,700 year-end 2025 and broader industry expectations for elevated prices into 2026. Record Q3 demand/value data from the World Gold Council also support a still-strong backdrop. Main downside risks are a sharp unwind of risk premiums, stronger dollar/real yields, or a speculative washout after the rapid run-up. Even allowing for those, staying above $3,200 by the final 2025 close looked highly likely.

As of 2025-11-09, Z.ai appears to be a credible but clearly non-leading contender for the top Chatbot Arena spot by 2025-12-31. Available pre-cutoff evidence suggests its GLM-4.5 family was strong—often described as roughly 3rd-tier globally in mid/late 2025 coverage—but not actually the Arena leader. Meanwhile the top of Chatbot Arena was highly volatile and dominated by OpenAI, Google, xAI, Anthropic, and sometimes DeepSeek, with multiple major labs frequently rotating through #1 and extensive private testing favoring those incumbents. That means there is some path for Z.ai to jump to the top via a new late-year release or fast Elo movement, but it is competing against several stronger, better-resourced labs already clustered near the top. The tie-break rule also slightly hurts Z.ai because its name is alphabetically late. Net: possible but unlikely, around a 12% chance.

AICO appears to be a small indigenous party that at most plausibly contests the tiny indigenous-reserved Senate constituency, with evidence pointing to only 1–2 indigenous seats overall, while major blocs like Historic Pact and traditional parties were winning double-digit Senate seats in 2022. Available pre-cutoff reporting discusses AICO selecting candidates, but provides no sign it is scaling into a nationwide top-seat contender by 2026. The alphabetical tiebreak slightly helps in an extremely unlikely tie, but not enough to offset the huge structural disadvantage versus large coalitions/parties.

As of 2025-11-09, Bitcoin had not apparently hit $90k during the already-open resolution window, but it had come fairly close: Reuters/CNBC reports show a record high above $125k in early October, a sharp liquidation-driven drop to about $104.8k on Oct. 10-11, and a brief break below $100k to about $99,954 on Nov. 4, with BTC around $101.6k on Nov. 9. That leaves the trigger only ~11% below spot and only ~10% below the recent low. For BTC, especially on Binance 1-minute candles, that is very reachable over ~7 more weeks. Evidence of heavy put-buying, risk-off macro pressure, miner selling, and recent extreme volatility all support meaningful downside risk. Offsetting that, BTC was still in a strong 2025 bull regime with institutional/ETF demand and had already survived a major washout without printing $90k. Net: slightly below even odds, but substantial chance of a wick or broader selloff reaching $90k by Dec. 31.

Wikipedia’s Top women section as of the cutoff shows Kateryna Lagno ranked #9 on the 1 Nov 2025 FIDE list, at 2517. As of the 2025-11-04 Wikipedia revision, Bobby Finke is listed as the men’s long-course 1500m freestyle world-record holder at 14:30.67. That means the record had already survived the biggest 2025 long-course target meet (the Singapore World Championships, held in July/August 2025 per the search results) and was still intact just before the forecast date. The remaining period from Nov. 9 to mid-November and early December is especially favorable to the record standing, because the elite swimming calendar is typically dominated by short-course racing late in the year, with few prime long-course record opportunities. By early February 2026 there is simply more elapsed time for a surprise long-course swim or an early-season attempt, so I move somewhat closer to 0.5, but I still think Finke remaining the listed holder is clearly more likely than not.

As of the Wikipedia cutoff page (List of infectious diseases, revision 2025-10-01), the Enterobiasis (Pinworm infection) row has a blank vaccine field, which implies no vaccine listed/developed at that time. The single additional search turned up reviews and treatment/background pages, but no sign of an approved or near-term Enterobius vermicularis vaccine program. Pinworm is common but usually treatable with existing antiparasitic drugs and hygiene measures, so the incentive and visibility for rapid vaccine development are low. Therefore a change from Wikipedia’s current 'No' status to 'Yes' within days or weeks looks very unlikely; I raise the probability only slightly by February because the longer horizon gives a bit more time for either discovery of an existing overlooked vaccine or an unexpected update.

The one web check added context that fundamentals were solid (strong underwriting, low Q3 catastrophe losses, favorable reserve development), which supports the stock generally, but those positives were likely already reflected in the post-earnings jump. For later dates, stock-level predictability falls and probabilities should drift back toward 0.5, so I move from 0.40 at the 1-week horizon to 0.43 by early December and 0.46 by early February.
